creator: will@physics.purdue.edu
text:
On Sat, Mar 31, 2001 at 04:07:32PM -0500, Trevor Johnson wrote:
> I have been told that it was done in the past, periodically.  Regardless,
> I wish it would not be done any more.

It has been done periodically.  But Peter's deed last October was the
first time in a few years.

> I was told that conserving disk space was not the reason for the removal
> of the attics.  Rather, the justification was that it would save time.
> Personally, I would rather have the actual attic files restored.  That
> would make it possible (modulo chaos from files which were purged but
> since have undergone a "cvs add") for someone to check out a copy of the
> ports tree as it was in the past, or to read the history.  Being able to
> do these things is the whole point of using CVS.  The files in
> http://people.freebsd.org/~trevor/ports/attics-all.tar.bz2 closely
> correspond to the ones which were removed.  Adding them all back would
> take on the order of 160 to 240 MB, to my (vague) recollection.

Could you do a tar tvfy on that and post the list somewhere?  I don't
think it's justified to put them back when history for said files
already exists, just in other locations.  Blank files would be fine though.=
..

--=20
wca
